title: MIST
description: Eval-driven infrastructure for AI systems. MatchSpec, InferMux, SchemaFlux, TokenTrace.
author: Grey Newell

builtWith.name: swe-bench-fast
builtWith.description: SWE-bench eval harness with native ARM64 containers. 6.3x test runner speedup on Apple Silicon and AWS Graviton.

tools.name: matchspec
tools.description: Eval framework for AI systems. Define datasets, write graders, run suites, gate deployments on passing thresholds.

tools.name: infermux
tools.description: Inference router for AI systems. Load balance across providers, fail over automatically, cost-optimize model calls.

tools.name: schemaflux
tools.description: Structured data compiler. Parse markdown and frontmatter, run 12-pass IR pipeline, emit typed output.

tools.name: tokentrace
tools.description: Observability for AI inference. Track cost, latency, and quality across your stack. Alert on regressions.

tools.name: mist-go
tools.description: Shared Go library implementing the MIST protocol. Protocol, transport, metrics, circuit breaking, checkpointing. Zero external dependencies.

useCases.title: AI Agents
useCases.description: Cascading errors, context rot, and agents that ignore instructions. What the benchmarks don't show about production agents.

useCases.title: Model Harnesses
useCases.description: Bad data, silent failures, and catastrophic forgetting. The real problems behind fine-tuning that tutorials skip.

useCases.title: RL Environments
useCases.description: Reward hacking, reproducibility crises, and the debugging abyss. Why RL training fails and what's missing from the toolchain.
